# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

imports:
- path: projects/dm/global

resources: 
- name: my-gcp-app
  type: projects/dm/global/baseApp:v1
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    
- name: vm-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/my-gcp-project/zones/us-central1-a
    machineType: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/my-gcp-project/zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks: 
      - deviceName: boot
        type: PERSISTENT
        boot: true
        autoDelete: true
        initializeParams: 
          sourceImage: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
    networkInterfaces: 
      - network: https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/my-gcp-project/global/networks/default
    
- name: sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    databaseVersion: POSTGRES_9_6
    settings:
      tier: db-custom-1-3840
      ipConfiguration:
        ipv4Enabled: true
    region: us-central1
    project: my-gcp-project
    instanceId: sql-instance
  metadata: 
    items: 
      - key: foo
        value: bar
        
- name: storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.bucket
  properties:
    location: US
    storageClass: STANDARD
    
- name: lb-instance
  type: compute.v1.targetPool
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    instances: 
      - https://www.googleapis.<｜begin▁of▁sentence｜>.compute/v1/projects/my-gcp-project/zones/us-central1-a/instances/vm-instance
